description_long: This is a World War II-era fighter airplane, visually modeled after the iconic Supermarine Spitfire used by the Royal Air Force. The aircraft features a streamlined fuselage with a single propeller engine at the nose, elliptical low-mounted wings adorned with roundel insignias, and a camouflage paint scheme of brown and green with light underbelly. The cockpit is positioned just behind the engine, enclosed with a clear canopy, and the tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear is in a taildragger configuration, with two main wheels under the wings and a small tail wheel. Distinctive markings, such as the RAF roundels and squadron codes, are visible on the wings and fuselage.
description: A WWII-era fighter airplane with elliptical low-mounted wings, camouflage paint, and RAF insignia, featuring a single propeller engine and enclosed cockpit.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the elliptical wings, roundel insignias, and camouflage pattern.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the propeller, cockpit canopy, and taildragger landing gear.
description_view_2: Front view emphasizing the propeller, nose, and symmetrical wing shape.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the tail section, vertical stabilizer, and fuselage markings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, elliptical planform with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a three-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed gear under the wings and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy just behind the engine.
